title: Resume Writing
description: Craft a resume that grabs recruiters’ attention and boosts your interview chances with these practical, beginner-friendly tips for 2025 and beyond.

tips: Think of your resume as your personal marketing brochure — it should sell you in under 10 seconds.
Start by writing down all your experiences, then trim based on job requirements.
Use a master resume (with everything), and create tailored versions for each job application.
Stick to modern, clean fonts (Arial, Calibri, Roboto, Open Sans) and 11–12 pt size.
Use clear headings: Summary, Education, Experience, Skills, Projects, Certifications.
Keep margins at 0.75–1 inch; use enough white space to avoid looking cluttered.
Avoid using photos, tables, or fancy graphics — they confuse Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS).
Use tools like Canva, Novoresume, Zety, or Resume.io for easy, beautiful templates.
Add a clear headline or title (e.g., 'Aspiring Data Analyst | Python | Excel | SQL').
Include a professional summary: 3–4 lines describing your goals and what you bring.
List internships, volunteer work, college projects — anything that shows skills in action.
Use bullet points (max 5 per job/project), and start each one with a power verb.
Mention awards, hackathons, campus leadership, or club roles — they show initiative.
Tailor your resume for each role using keywords from the job post.
Separate your technical (e.g., JavaScript, Tableau) and soft skills (e.g., leadership, teamwork).
Use tools like Jobscan or ResumeWorded to test your resume's keyword match.
Mention tools you’ve actually used: VS Code, Figma, Trello, MySQL, ChatGPT, etc.
Add 2–3 college or personal projects with a title, tools used, and your contribution.
Explain results: Did it solve a real problem? Did it win any prize or get noticed?
Link to GitHub or demo if available — it builds credibility instantly.
Highlight teamwork, problem-solving, or creativity used in the project.
Use ChatGPT to rephrase or summarize your experience clearly and professionally.
Use Grammarly to fix grammar and spelling issues instantly.
Use Rezi or Kickresume for AI-based formatting and score checks.
Let AI help tailor your resume for different jobs by adjusting keywords and tone.
✅ Use a professional-looking email like firstname.lastname@gmail.com.
✅ Include a LinkedIn profile — keep it updated with matching content.
✅ Use action words like 'Led', 'Initiated', 'Analyzed', 'Built', 'Improved'.
❌ Don’t include your full address — city and pin code are enough.
❌ Don’t lie or exaggerate — background verification can spot this quickly.
❌ Avoid outdated terms like 'Objective: To work in a challenging environment...'.
Proofread twice — read it aloud or ask a mentor to review.
Save as PDF to preserve formatting across devices.
Use a filename like 'Kishan_Mistry_Resume2025.pdf'.
Double-check hyperlinks (LinkedIn, GitHub, portfolio) before sending.
Update your resume regularly, even when not job-hunting.
